mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds +0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60d returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust domestic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or 20d forward returns are negative in >55% of co-growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that outweigh demand, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5d returns in >60% of cases, or the regime filter adds no incremental predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the CPI above 250.0 forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Markets price in inflation passively, yielding positive 20d returns in >65% of high-CPI periods, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance, or 60d returns are negative in >55% of strong-payroll and low-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth signals broad economic strength and corporate earnings expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict 60d returns with Rank IC above 0.04, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average indicates a supply-side slowdown that depresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 rallies in >60% of periods when INDPRO drops below its 12-month MA, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.
